verdict_reasoning: SRC is a cytoplasmic Src-family tyrosine kinase with no intrinsic membrane anchor. However, it undergoes N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, enabling transient association with the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and/or other endomembranes. SRC also cycles between cytoplasm and membrane compartments during signaling, particularly when recruited to receptor-family complexes at the PM via SH2/SH3 interactions with anchored partners (e.g., receptor tyrosine kinases, integrins, tetraspanins). The protein body itself does not have a mechanism to stably access the outer leaflet; however, documented dual localization with a minority PM pool during active signaling qualifies as contextual surface-associated biology.
